sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Vultr Collaborates with AMD, Broadcom and Juniper Networks to Pioneer New GPU Data Center Architecture
feed.summary: Vultr has announced a strategic four-way collaboration with AMD, Broadcom, and Juniper Networks to create a new GPU data center architecture. This initiative includes expanding Vultr's Chicago data center with an AMD GPU supercompute cluster powered by ROCm™ open software. The collaboration aims to provide enterprises and AI innovators with high-performance, scalable, and secure cloud GPU infrastructure for global AI training and inference workloads

feed.title: Vultr, AMD, Broadcom, Juniper Team for ‘AI Innovation’
feed.summary: Vultr is collaborating with AMD, Broadcom, and Juniper Networks to create an open ecosystem for artificial intelligence, aiming to accelerate AI deployment for enterprises. This partnership brings together essential components for cloud-fueled AI, benefiting Vultr's MSPs and VARs by enabling GPU-accelerated workloads. The new alliance involves AMD supplying Instinct MI300X GPUs and ROCm software, Broadcom providing Ethernet network adapters, and Juniper contributing AI-optimized Ethernet switches, with Vultr expanding its Chicago data center to support this initiative.

feed.title: Report: Apple working with Broadcom to develop custom chipset for AI
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly collaborating with Broadcom Inc. to develop a custom server processor, codenamed “Baltra,” for AI features in its iOS operating system, with a targeted launch in 2026. This move aligns with Apple's strategy of using proprietary chips for on-device AI and Broadcom's expertise in semiconductor interconnect designs. The project likely leverages Broadcom's advanced packaging technologies for faster chip-to-chip communications.

feed.title: Lenovo, HP And Dell Are Betting Big On A PC Refresh In 2025 (So Is The Channel)
feed.summary: Major PC manufacturers like Lenovo, HP, and Dell, along with their channel partners, are highly optimistic about a significant PC refresh in 2025. This anticipation is driven by several factors, including the Windows 10 end-of-life, the increasing demand for AI-capable PCs, and a large installed base of aging computers purchased during the COVID-19 pandemic. While some industry analysts predict the full surge of AI PC sales might extend into 2026, the consensus among these tech giants and solution providers points to 2025 as a pivotal year for PC market recovery, especially in the commercial sector.

feed.title: 1 Spectacular Artificial Intelligence (AI) ETF to Buy With $40 During the S&P 500 Bull Market
feed.summary: The article highlights the iShares Future AI and Tech ETF (ARTY) as a promising investment for exposure to the booming AI market, especially given the S&P 500's strong performance driven by technology stocks. It details the ETF's highly concentrated portfolio, featuring top AI companies like Nvidia and Palantir, and suggests it could be a valuable addition to a balanced investment portfolio despite its short track record.

feed.title: Broadcom's AI Chip Leadership Puts Pressure on Nvidia and Marvell, Analysts Say
feed.summary: Broadcom's stock surged after reporting strong Q4 adjusted EPS results and raising its dividend, driven by its leadership in AI custom Application-Specific Integrated Circuits (ASICs). Analysts upgraded Broadcom's price targets, highlighting its expanding AI opportunities and advanced 3nm silicon technology, which puts it ahead of competitors like Nvidia and Marvell. The company anticipates significant growth in its AI revenues, expecting to reach $30 billion by calendar 2027 based on strong demand from hyperscalers.

feed.title: A decade ago, she took over a struggling chipmaker—now she's worth $1 billion: 'Everything takes time'
feed.summary: Lisa Su took over Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) a decade ago when the company was struggling, with its stock at $3 per share and significant staff cuts. Under her leadership, AMD's market cap has grown to over $205 billion and her net worth has reached $1.3 billion. Su, named Time's CEO of the Year for 2024, attributes her success to a long-term strategy focused on high-quality products, customer trust, and simplified operations, emphasizing that "everything takes time."

feed.title: iShares Future AI & Tech ETF Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the holdings of the iShares Future AI & Tech ETF (ARTY), which was established on June 26, 2018, and focuses on global companies contributing to AI technologies. The ETF manages $3224.19 million in assets and holds 52 positions, with top holdings including Marvell Technology Inc., Advanced Micro Devices Inc., and Micron Technology Inc. The report also provides a list of recent changes in the ETF's holdings, predominantly showing movements in various cash currencies.

feed.title: Invesco Large Cap Growth ETF Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the holdings of the Invesco Large Cap Growth ETF (PWB), which was launched in 2005 and has $2043.34 million in assets under management across 51 holdings. The ETF tracks an index focused on large-cap stocks with growth characteristics, listing its top holdings such as Micron Technology, Applied Materials, and Lam Research. It also provides a comprehensive record of recent changes in its portfolio, including additions and deletions of shares for various companies.

feed.title: First Trust Debuts Defense, Semiconductor ETFs
feed.summary: First Trust has launched two new UCITS ETFs: the First Trust Indxx Global Aerospace & Defense UCITS ETF (MISL) and the First Trust Bloomberg Global Semiconductor Supply Chain UCITS ETF (CHPS). These ETFs are listed on the London Stock Exchange with TERs of 0.65% and 0.60% respectively, expanding First Trust's UCITS ETF range to 36. MISL focuses on companies deriving at least 50% of revenues from aerospace or defense sectors, while CHPS allocates 70% to semiconductor manufacturing and 30% to semiconductor device companies.

feed.title: Channel Brief: Onapsis Plans to Double Revenue With New CRO
feed.summary: Onapsis aims to double its revenue by 2026, driven by its new Chief Revenue Officer, Paul Kleinschnitz, focusing on product roadmap execution and partnerships. Other tech news includes Grip Security partnering with SailPoint, Tim Wintrip becoming CEO of Lemongrass, Vultr raising $333 million, and the U.S. considering a ban on TP-Link routers due to national security concerns. The article also touches on the Biden administration's overhaul of the H1-B visa program.

feed.title: Nvidia's Blackwell chip could push the company into a new stratosphere as the AI revolution continues
feed.summary: Nvidia's Blackwell chip is poised to further propel the company's growth in the AI sector, with analysts calling it a "beast" and an "exponential leap forward." Despite potential future competition in inference workloads, Blackwell is expected to dominate the AI training market and contribute significantly to Nvidia's record sales, even as demand continues to outstrip supply for several quarters.

feed.title: YieldMax Dorsey Wright Hybrid 5 Income ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:FIVY)
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information on the YieldMax Dorsey Wright Hybrid 5 Income ETF (FIVY), including its current price, and key statistics like market capitalization, assets under management, and dividend yield. It outlines the ETF's investment strategy, which involves tracking an index of five stocks and five affiliated ETFs using synthetic covered call strategies. The article also lists recent dividend declarations and its top holdings, such as YieldMax options income ETFs for GOOGL, AMD, PLTR, MRNA, and RDDT.

feed.title: Micron Set for Biggest Drop Since 2020 on Sluggish Sales Outlook
feed.summary: Micron Technology Inc. experienced its largest stock drop in over four years after its revenue forecast for the fiscal second quarter missed analyst projections significantly. The company attributes this downturn to sluggish demand in the smartphone and personal computer markets, despite growth in AI. Micron's sales are estimated at $7.9 billion, well below the $8.99 billion analyst estimate, with profit also falling short of expectations.

feed.title: COYY Stock Price | GraniteShares YieldBOOST COIN ETF
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information about the GraniteShares YieldBOOST COIN ETF (COYY), including its current stock price, trading exchange, and key financial statistics such as dividend yield and expense ratio. It also features news and analysis related to COYY and its underlying assets, like Coinbase, along with technical analysis indicators.

feed.title: Is It Worth Investing in Advanced Micro (AMD) Based on Wall Street's Bullish Views?
feed.summary: This article examines the reliability of Wall Street analyst recommendations for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), highlighting that while AMD has a strong "Buy" average brokerage recommendation (ABR), investors should be cautious due to potential analyst biases. It contrasts the ABR with the Zacks Rank, a proprietary stock rating tool, suggesting that the Zacks Rank offers a more objective and timely indicator for investment decisions based on earnings estimate revisions. The article concludes by recommending that investors use the Zacks Rank to validate their research, noting AMD's current Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Co-Leads $3.5 Billion Financing Round for Vultr to Boost AI and Cloud Infrastructure
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) co-led a $3.5 billion growth financing round for Vultr, a leading provider of cloud infrastructure and AI solutions. This investment aims to accelerate Vultr's global expansion and AI infrastructure capabilities, utilizing AMD Instinct accelerators. The move highlights AMD's strategic role in integrating AI into its semiconductor solutions and supporting high-performance computing for AI development and deployment.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Stock Pops on Bullish Coverage
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock surged by 4.6% after Rosenblatt initiated bullish coverage with a "buy" rating and a $250 price target, citing expanding market share in CPUs and GPUs. Despite the rally, AMD is still down 15.3% this year and faces resistance at its 20-day moving average and the $130 level. Options traders remain optimistic, with a high call/put volume ratio indicating strong investor confidence in the semiconductor giant.

feed.title: 2025 Crypto Outlook: Eight Themes To Watch
feed.summary: Alex Tapscott, managing director of the Digital Asset Group, shares his 2025 crypto and AI outlook. He highlights the "Trump trade" benefiting crypto, anticipating regulatory clarity and institutional adoption under a pro-crypto administration. The report also discusses Ethereum's overlooked strength in enterprise adoption and Nvidia's dominance in the booming AI chip market, predicting significant disruptions across various sectors due to AI.

feed.title: Cipher Mining Stock: Capitalizing On Bitcoin, AI, And HPC Trends (Upgrade) (NASDAQ:CIFR)
feed.summary: Cipher Mining Inc. (CIFR) is strategically expanding its Bitcoin mining and high-performance computing (HPC) infrastructure, aiming for 23.5 EH/s by 2025 by leveraging low-cost energy and operational efficiencies. The company's growth plan includes significant capacity increases in Texas with new facilities like Black Pearl and Stingray, which are expected to boost mining efficiency and computational power. Despite potential cash burn, CIFR's competitive production costs and high gross margins position it favorably to benefit from the current cryptocurrency bull market, leading to an upgraded "Buy" rating, though investors should prepare for high volatility.

feed.title: Nvidia Supplier Ibiden Weighs Faster Expansion for AI Demand
feed.summary: Ibiden Co., a key supplier of chip package substrates for Nvidia, is considering accelerating its production capacity expansion due to robust demand from the AI sector. CEO Koji Kawashima indicated that current expansion plans might not be sufficient to meet customer needs, prompting discussions for further investment. This expansion comes as Ibiden's shares rose and despite past reliance on Intel, the company is seeing increased demand driven by AI chips used by Nvidia and potentially other chipmakers.

feed.title: Returns On Capital Signal Tricky Times Ahead For Intchains Group (NASDAQ:ICG)
feed.summary: Intchains Group (NASDAQ:ICG) shows a declining Return on Capital Employed (ROCE), currently at 3.8% which is below the Semiconductor industry average. While the company's revenue and assets are growing, suggesting reinvestment for future growth, the ROCE has fallen significantly from 17% four years ago. The stock has also decreased by 25% over the last year, indicating potential opportunities for astute investors despite the challenging ROCE trend.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says ‘Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Is A Buy, And I Think It Will Continue To Perform Well’
feed.summary: Jim Cramer has endorsed Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) as a strong buy, citing its performance and strategic positioning in AI. The article highlights Amazon's role across the AI stack, its partnership with Anthropic, and discusses Cramer's investment tips for 2025 including avoiding "pigs get slaughtered" mentality, holding through tough markets, and staging buys.

feed.title: Unbelievable: How China’s Outsmarting US Chip Ban to Dominate AI
feed.summary: Despite US chip embargoes aimed at stifling its AI progress, China has innovated strategies to advance its AI capabilities. The country is focusing on local chip manufacturing, developing efficient code for smaller AI models, and leveraging cloud services and open-source models as workarounds. These efforts, supported by national strategies and significant investments, have allowed China to continue its rapid progress in AI, turning the embargo into a catalyst for self-reliance and innovation.

feed.title: F5 to Supercharge AI Application Delivery for Service Providers and Enterprises with NVIDIA BlueField-3 DPUs
feed.summary: F5 has announced the availability of BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes, an AI application delivery and security solution designed for service providers and large enterprises, leveraging NVIDIA BlueField-3 DPUs. This solution aims to accelerate, secure, and streamline data traffic for large-scale AI infrastructures by optimizing data center resource utilization and enhancing AI application performance. It is particularly beneficial for telco clouds, 5G infrastructures, and high-demand AI use cases, offering improved control, security, and efficiency.

feed.title: Vera Bradley aims to follow the Abercrombie playbook with a rebrand of its 2000s-favorite quilted bags
feed.summary: Vera Bradley is undergoing a significant rebrand aimed at revitalizing the company by targeting 35-to-55-year-old shoppers, moving beyond its signature patterned quilted bags. CEO Jackie Ardrey is leading "Project Restoration," inspired by successful rebrands like Abercrombie, to bring the $470 million business back to prominence with a new product line and spokesperson Zooey Deschanel. The rebrand explicitly avoids a pure nostalgia play or targeting Gen Z, focusing instead on a more influential customer demographic.

feed.title: MarketVector Reaches All-Time High of USD 50 Billion in Assets Under Management
feed.summary: MarketVector Indexes has achieved an all-time high of USD 50 billion in total assets under management (AUM) linked to its licensed indexes, driven significantly by its semiconductor index family with over USD 25 billion in AUM. This milestone highlights the company's growth, institutional credibility, and innovation, particularly in thematic and digital asset indexes, reinforced by strategic partnerships and investor interest in AI and digital assets. MarketVector attributes its success to its team's dedication and client trust, positioning itself as a leader in the indexing industry.

feed.title: Jabil Acquires Mikros Technologies to Support Demand for Liquid Cooling in AI Data Centers
feed.summary: Jabil Inc. has acquired Mikros Technologies LLC, a leader in liquid cooling solutions, to enhance its thermal management capabilities for AI data centers and other high-power density systems. The acquisition, completed on October 1st, will allow Jabil to address the growing demand for efficient cooling in industries such as artificial intelligence, energy storage, and electric vehicles. Mikros Technologies' proprietary microchannel liquid cooling designs will complement Jabil's existing data center solutions.

feed.title: Keysight Introduces Chiplet PHY Designer for Simulating D2D to D2D PHY IP Supporting the UCIe™ Standard
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies introduces Chiplet PHY Designer, a new electronic design automation (EDA) tool for simulating die-to-die (D2D) interconnect performance in chiplets. This tool is the first in the industry to provide in-depth modeling and simulation capabilities for verifying designs against the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) standard, which is critical for heterogenous and 3D integrated circuit designs. It aims to accelerate validation and improve predictive virtual prototyping for chiplet subsystems.

feed.title: Micron Technology sees largest gain in nearly 13 years
feed.summary: Micron Technology experienced its largest single-day gain in nearly 13 years, with shares rising 15% to $109.88, driven by surprisingly strong sales and profit forecasts. The rosy outlook is attributed to a boom in AI spending, with high-bandwidth memory products—crucial for AI systems—already sold out for 2024 and 2025. The company's recent fiscal fourth-quarter results also beat estimates, further cementing its strong position in the chip market.

feed.title: Micron (Virginia)
feed.summary: NIST has awarded Micron Virginia Semiconductor Manufacturing LLC direct funding under the CHIPS and Science Act to expand and modernize its chip fabrication facility in Manassas, Virginia. This investment aims to onshore Micron’s 1-alpha DRAM technology and strengthen U.S. economic and national security by bolstering a reliable domestic supply of critical memory chips, especially for automotive, industrial, and aerospace and defense sectors. The project will create jobs and secure lasting production of legacy memory technology.
feed.source: National Institute of Standards and Technology (.gov)
feed.source_domain: National Institute of Standards and Technology (.gov)

feed.title: Nixxy CEO Granger Whitelaw Resigns, Successor Search Begins
feed.summary: Granger Whitelaw has resigned from his roles as a member of the Board of Directors and as Chief Executive Officer of Nixxy, Inc. His CEO resignation is effective on December 31, 2024. The company has begun the search for a new CEO, and the departure is not due to any disagreement with the company.

feed.title: COMPUTEX 2024 News
feed.summary: NVIDIA's COMPUTEX 2024 news features CEO Jensen Huang's "Accelerate Everything" message and a series of announcements focusing on AI, including digital human microservices, NIM for model deployment, and supercharged Ethernet networking. The company is collaborating with the computer industry to build AI factories and data centers, and bringing AI assistants to GeForce RTX AI PCs, highlighting its pervasive role in advancing AI across various industries like robotics, healthcare, and industrial digitalization.

feed.title: The 10 Biggest Nvidia News Stories Of 2024 (So Far)
feed.summary: This article covers the 10 biggest Nvidia news stories of 2024 to date, highlighting the company's rapid expansion and continued dominance in AI computing. Key developments include strategic software startup acquisitions, plans for new GPU and CPU architectures, and significant financial achievements like surpassing Intel in annual revenue and briefly becoming the world's most valuable company. Nvidia's focus on a "full-stack" computing approach, encompassing hardware, software, and services, underpins its success amidst growing competition.

feed.title: Teradyne's Bright Forecast Sparks Investor Interest
feed.summary: Teradyne, a semiconductor-testing leader, forecasts a strong fourth quarter, with profit and revenue figures expected to surpass Wall Street's predictions due to rising demand for its technology, particularly from memory chip makers and cloud computing firms focused on AI. This positive outlook has energized investors, leading to a 2.5% increase in shares. The company is also diversifying into robotics, demonstrating a commitment to innovation beyond its core semiconductor testing business.

feed.title: Rambus Unveils Industry-First Complete Chipsets for Next-Generation DDR5 MRDIMMs and RDIMMs to Deliver Breakthrough Performance for Data Center and AI
feed.summary: Rambus has introduced industry-first, complete memory interface chipsets for Gen5 DDR5 RDIMMs and next-generation DDR5 MRDIMMs. These new products are designed to deliver unparalleled bandwidth and memory capacity for data center and AI workloads, operating at speeds up to 12,800 MT/s. The chipsets include a Gen5 Registering Clock Driver, Multiplexed Registering Clock Driver, Multiplexed Data Buffer, and a second-generation server Power Management IC.

feed.title: Nvidia, AMD Lead The Pack, Broadcom Stable, Intel Falters: Analyst's Latest Take
feed.summary: KeyBanc analyst John Vinh provided a mixed outlook for the semiconductor sector, with Nvidia and AMD showing strong performance and positive catalysts. Conversely, Qualcomm and Synaptics were downgraded due to increased competition and limited growth, while Intel, Micron, Marvell, and Monolithic Power Systems faced negative findings. Broadcom's stable outlook is tempered by potential future competition from Apple.

feed.title: Cadence Unveils Palladium Z3 and Protium X3 Systems to Usher in a New Era of Accelerated Verification, Software Development and Digital Twins
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems, Inc. announced its new Palladium Z3 Emulation and Protium X3 FPGA Prototyping systems, designed to accelerate verification and software development for complex SoCs. These systems offer over twice the capacity and are 1.5 times faster than previous generations, supporting advanced applications like generative AI, mobile, and automotive. The Palladium Z3 features a custom emulation processor and domain-specific apps, while the Protium X3 provides faster bring-up times for software validation, with both systems supporting massive gate designs up to 48 billion.

feed.title: IBM, GlobalFoundries settle legal dispute over chip technology
feed.summary: IBM Corp. and GlobalFoundries Inc. have resolved a multi-year legal dispute concerning chip technology and manufacturing agreements. The settlement opens doors for potential future collaborations between the two companies. The dispute stemmed from IBM's accusation of breach of contract by GlobalFoundries and GlobalFoundries' counterclaim of trade secret sharing by IBM.

feed.title: CES 2025: HP’s OMEN AI Casts a Spell on Gaming, Optimizing Performance with a Single Click
feed.summary: HP announced new OMEN and HyperX gaming products at CES 2025, including the OMEN AI, the world's first AI-driven 1-click performance optimization tool. The OMEN MAX 16 is introduced as HP's most powerful gaming laptop, featuring NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs and enhanced thermal management. Additionally, HP unveiled the OMEN 32x Smart Gaming Monitor with built-in Google TV and the customizable HyperX Pulsefire Saga mice series.

feed.title: CES 2025: Shaping the Future of Play
feed.summary: Josephine Tan, Senior Vice President & General Manager, Gaming Personal Systems at HP, details new gaming innovations unveiled at CES 2025. The article highlights new OMEN laptops and desktops, enhanced Victus laptops, new OMEN monitors with high refresh rates, and precise HyperX mice. These products are designed to offer immersive, high-performance, and personalized gaming experiences, supported by HP's ongoing strategic alliance with Riot Games.

feed.title: NVIDIA Launches AI Foundation Models for RTX AI PCs
feed.summary: NVIDIA announced the launch of foundation models for RTX AI PCs, designed to power digital humans, content creation, productivity, and development. These models, offered as NVIDIA NIM microservices, are accelerated by new GeForce RTX 50 Series GPUs, featuring significant AI performance and supporting FP4 compute for enhanced inference. NVIDIA is collaborating with top PC manufacturers to integrate these NIM-ready RTX AI PCs, emphasizing the company's commitment to advancing AI accessibility for developers and enthusiasts.

feed.title: NVIDIA Blackwell GeForce RTX 50 Series Opens New World of AI Computer Graphics
feed.summary: NVIDIA has announced its new GeForce RTX 50 Series Desktop and Laptop GPUs, powered by the Blackwell architecture, which aim to revolutionize AI computer graphics. These GPUs offer significant performance increases, up to 2x over the previous generation, through advancements in neural shaders, DLSS 4 with Multi Frame Generation, and AI-driven rendering. The new series also introduces AI-powered tools for creators, autonomous game characters, and strengthens AI capabilities for PCs.

feed.title: Nvidia's keynote at CES a positive catalyst for Super Micro Computer - Analyst
feed.summary: Lynx Equity has reiterated a $60 price target for Super Micro Computer (SMCI), viewing Nvidia's CES keynote as a positive catalyst. The firm believes Super Micro's lead in liquid-cooled technology positions it well for the demands of Nvidia's next-gen GB300 platform and other AI server needs. Although considered speculative, the company's potential for a debt-financed capital raise could benefit inventory and AI server expansion.

feed.title: Intel Extends Leadership in AI PCs and Edge Computing at CES 2025
feed.summary: At CES 2025, Intel unveiled new Intel Core Ultra (Series 2) processors, including the 200V series with Intel vPro for businesses, and 200HX and H series for creators and gamers, all featuring cutting-edge AI enhancements and performance improvements. Intel also introduced a new suite of processors for edge computing, showcasing significant performance gains in AI workloads compared to previous generations and competitors. The availability of these new systems is set to begin in January and February 2025, with further releases planned for the first half of the year.

feed.title: UniFirst Shares Skyrocket As Cintas Reveals $275 Per Share Acquisition Offer
feed.summary: Cintas Corporation has made a public offer to acquire UniFirst Corporation for $275 per share in cash, valuing UniFirst at approximately $5.3 billion. This offer represents a significant premium to UniFirst's recent closing prices. Despite Cintas emphasizing the strategic fit and operational synergies, UniFirst's board has repeatedly declined to engage in discussions, prompting Cintas to publicize its proposal to UniFirst shareholders.

feed.title: Absci and AMD Team Up to Revolutionize Drug Discovery with AI
feed.summary: Absci and AMD have announced a pivotal collaboration to revolutionize drug discovery using AI. AMD is investing $20 million in Absci, leveraging AMD Instinct™ accelerators and ROCm™ software to power Absci's AI-driven drug discovery processes and aiming to accelerate the creation of biologics through high-performance computing. This partnership is expected to reduce infrastructure costs, speed up innovation cycles, and enhance the performance of Absci's proprietary models, such as IgDesign1.

feed.title: AMD to invest $20m in Absci to boost AI drug discovery tech
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is investing $20 million in Absci Corporation, a generative AI drug creation company, to enhance its AI drug discovery capabilities. AMD will provide its Instinct accelerators and ROCm software to support Absci’s technology, particularly in de novo antibody design models. This partnership aims to accelerate the development of next-generation antibody therapeutics, leveraging AI's growing role in transforming the pharmaceutical industry.

feed.title: AMD invests $20M in AI-powered drug discovery company Absci
feed.summary: AMD has invested $20 million in Absci Corp., an AI-powered company focused on drug discovery, and will also partner with them on research. Absci, which uses artificial intelligence to identify therapeutic proteins and epitopes, will shift some of its workloads to AMD's Instinct AI processors and utilize AMD's ROCm software. This collaboration aims to enhance Absci's AI models and develop new hardware and software optimized for the healthcare sector, particularly in drug discovery.

feed.title: Arizona will host third CHIPS R&D flagship facility
feed.summary: Arizona State University Research Park in Tempe has been selected as the site for the third CHIPS for America research and development facility, focusing on semiconductor prototyping and packaging. This facility, which will be operational by Q4 2028, will involve the highest financial investment among the three R&D centers. It aims to bridge lab-to-production challenges by providing a manufacturing-like setting for diverse R&D activities, leveraging Arizona's existing microelectronics ecosystem.

feed.title: Dell picks AMD for business PCs
feed.summary: Dell Technologies will start using Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) chips for its business PCs for the first time, a move announced at CES in Las Vegas. This decision is seen as a blow to Intel's dominance in the PC chip market, especially in the more profitable commercial sector. AMD unveiled new Ryzen AI Max series processors and 9000 series desktop processors, claiming significant performance improvements for AI workloads, while competitors Intel and Qualcomm also showcased their AI-focused chips.

feed.title: Delta (DAL), AMD, Walgreens (WBA) ou Constellation Energy (CEG): qual ação comprar e qual pular
feed.summary: This article analyzes four major companies—Delta Air Lines (DAL), AMD, Walgreens (WBA), and Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG)—to guide investors on which stocks to buy and which to avoid. It recommends buying Delta and Constellation Energy due to strong financial performance and stable market positions, while advising to skip Walgreens and AMD due to ongoing restructuring challenges and competitive pressures, respectively. The analysis emphasizes considering financial health, growth potential, and individual risk tolerance before making investment decisions.

feed.title: S&P Global Upgrades Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) to ‘A’ Rating: AI Growth and Strong Market Share Propel Outlook
feed.summary: S&P Global has upgraded Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to an ‘A’ credit rating from ‘A-’, citing strong growth prospects driven by AI-accelerated computing chip sales and gains in the x86 product segment. The outlook remains stable, with projections for 13% revenue growth in 2024 and 24% in 2025, primarily from its data center segment. This upgrade reflects AMD's solid financial health and strategic positioning within the expanding AI market.

feed.title: Illumina and NVIDIA collaborate to decode biology and propel precision health
feed.summary: Illumina and NVIDIA are partnering to advance technology platforms for multiomic data analysis and interpretation. This collaboration aims to accelerate drug discovery and clinical development by combining Illumina's sequencing technologies and software with NVIDIA's AI tools, including foundation models and accelerated computing. The initial phase will focus on enabling Illumina's DRAGEN algorithms on NVIDIA GPUs and integrating NVIDIA's image processing tools into Illumina's Connected Software.

feed.title: Supermicro Empowers AI-driven Capabilities for Enterprise, Retail, and Edge Server Solutions
feed.summary: Supermicro is showcasing its latest AI server, storage, and edge computing solutions for the retail industry at the National Retail Federation (NRF) annual show, in collaboration with NVIDIA. These solutions aim to enhance retail operations, security, and efficiency by leveraging generative AI for personalized shopping, logistical automation, and shrinkage prevention. Supermicro highlights its broad portfolio of edge AI solutions and various server systems designed to deliver high-end performance at retail locations, demonstrating use cases like digital human customer service, AI shopping assistants, and video search for loss prevention.

feed.title: AMD: Trading at a 20% Discount, This Misunderstood Stock Is a Buy | Morningstar UK
feed.summary: Morningstar believes Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is undervalued, trading at a 20% discount to its fair value estimate despite a challenging 2024. The company is well-positioned in the lucrative AI chip market, second only to Nvidia, and its strong data center business is expected to drive significant growth. Morningstar maintains a "narrow economic moat" rating for AMD, citing its chip design expertise and its unique x86 instruction set architecture license.

feed.title: How Insider Selling Triggered Nauticus Robotics’ 33% Stock Slide
feed.summary: Nauticus Robotics Inc. experienced a 33% stock drop following news of its CEO selling 3,190 shares, closing at $2.07. Despite this, the company is pursuing significant contracts for 2025 and preparing its second Aquanaut vehicle for commercial deployment. While insider selling raises concerns, the stock showed some recovery in after-hours trading, indicating speculative interest.

feed.title: Analog Devices lands $105 million in federal CHIPS money for projects near Portland and Boston
feed.summary: Analog Devices has secured $105 million in federal CHIPS Act funding to upgrade its manufacturing facilities located near Portland, Oregon, and Boston. This funding positions Analog Devices as the third company to receive CHIPS Act money for projects in Oregon. The company's semiconductor chips are crucial for various industries, including automotive, industrial equipment, airplanes, and national defense.

feed.title: Tech Investing in 2025: Emerging Trends and Market Opportunities
feed.summary: In 2025, technology continues to fuel innovation and economic expansion, with AI, semiconductors, cloud computing, cybersecurity, and renewable energy offering significant investment opportunities. A diversified investment strategy is crucial to navigate this dynamic environment, as the global economy and supportive policies are expected to provide strong tailwinds for the tech sector. The article highlights key areas like semiconductors, nuclear energy, and cloud computing, suggesting thematic ETFs (like SMH and NUCL) as practical solutions for broad exposure while mitigating risk.

feed.title: TSMC has begun chip production in Arizona, company confirms
feed.summary: TSMC has confirmed it has begun producing advanced manufacturing chips in Phoenix, Arizona. This marks the first time 4-nanometer chips have been produced on American soil, a significant development for the U.S. semiconductor industry supported by the CHIPS Act. Despite potential political shifts, the company's grant under the Act has been finalized, and two more fabs are under construction.

feed.title: Should You Steer Clear of Intel and Enphase Stocks in 2025?
feed.summary: This article examines whether Intel (INTC) and Enphase Energy (ENPH) stocks, both of which have seen significant declines, represent a buying opportunity or should be avoided in 2025. Intel faces challenges from strong competition and slow AI adoption, while Enphase struggles with high interest rates and European market weakness. Both companies have strategies for recovery, but face considerable headwinds and have received cautious ratings from Wall Street analysts.

feed.title: Governor Hochul Announces Corning to Invest More Than $315 Million and Create up to 300 Jobs at Semiconductor Glass Manufacturing Facility in North Country
feed.summary: Governor Kathy Hochul announced that Corning Incorporated will invest up to $315 million in its Canton, St. Lawrence County facility, creating up to 130 new manufacturing jobs and 175 construction jobs. This investment will increase the production of High Purity Fused Silica and EXTREME Ultra Low Expansion glass, critical for advanced semiconductor manufacturing, and supports New York State's efforts to grow the semiconductor industry. Empire State Development is providing up to $7 million in Excelsior Jobs Program tax credits to support Corning's expansion.

feed.title: Advanced Semiconductor Packaging Market to Generate US$ 40.3 Billion in Revenue by 2031, Driven by the Surge in Demand for Miniaturized Electronics | Latest Report by TMR
feed.summary: The advanced semiconductor packaging market, valued at US$ 30.1 billion in 2022, is projected to reach US$ 40.3 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 5.2%. This growth is fueled by increasing demand for compact, high-performance electronic devices, driven by innovations in packaging technologies like 3D and wafer-level packaging, and the rising adoption of 5G, IoT, and AI applications. Key drivers include miniaturization trends, 5G technology, IoT and edge computing, automotive electronics, and AI/high-performance computing, with major companies investing in R&D and strategic partnerships.

feed.title: HPE Investigates After Alleged Data Breach
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is investigating claims by the cyberattack group IntelBroker of a data breach, which allegedly involved access to HPE's API, GitHub repositories, and source code. This is not the first time HPE has faced such incidents, with previous breaches in 2018 and 2021. While HPE has activated its cyber-response protocols and says there's no evidence of customer information involvement or operational impact, the validity and scope of the claims are still under investigation.

feed.title: Mercury to develop data processing subsystem for U.S. satellite program
feed.summary: Mercury Systems has secured a $24.5 million contract to develop a data processing and storage subsystem for a U.S. Department of Defense satellite program. The subsystem will utilize Mercury's commercial products, including its SCFE6933 processing board and RH304T solid-state data recorder, leveraging AMD Versal AI Core series FPGAs. This contract highlights Mercury's innovative approach to integrating commercial technologies for critical defense applications and reinforces its role in supporting national security missions.

feed.title: Tesla Price Target Bumped To $500 By Bank With 120x P/E Multiple Stemming From “Nebulous” Business
feed.summary: Investment bank Piper Sandler has raised its price target for Tesla shares to $500, citing the company's potential in "real-world" AI and future opportunities like Optimus robots, despite a mixed performance in 2024. The bank acknowledges that immediate financial estimates for Tesla are highly uncertain due to the nascent nature of these business segments, leading them to use a 120x fiscal year 2026 P/E valuation.

feed.title: BNDS Stock Price | Infrastructure Capital Bond Income ETF
feed.summary: This article provides detailed financial information for the Infrastructure Capital Bond Income ETF (BNDS), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NYSE), and key statistics like dividend yield and expense ratio. It also includes performance data, top holdings, technical analysis, and company profile. Investors looking for a bond ETF can use this data to assess BNDS's suitability for their portfolio.

feed.title: SMART Modular Add-In Cards Now Listed on CXL Consortium Integrators' List
feed.summary: SMART Modular Technologies announced its 4-DIMM and 8-DIMM CXL memory Add-in Cards (AICs) have successfully passed CXL 2.0 compliance testing and are now listed on the CXL Consortium's Integrators' List. This achievement highlights SMART Modular's dedication to industry standards and its role in advancing integrated memory technology to meet the growing demand for high-speed data processing. The company aims to provide cutting-edge solutions with enhanced performance and reliability.

feed.title: Analyst Praises AMD-Partner Absci's Practical Approach, Sees 118% Stock Upside
feed.summary: Needham analyst Gil Blum initiated coverage on Absci Corporation (ABSI) with a Buy rating and a $9 price target, citing the company's practical approach to AI-driven drug discovery. Absci, a partner with AMD, differentiates itself by generating large experimental datasets internally and focusing on highly optimized agents to reduce clinical failure. The company aims to develop first-in-class therapies and then partner for commercialization, also receiving milestone payments and royalties from collaborations with pharmaceutical companies like Merck and AstraZeneca.

feed.title: The Best Tech Stocks to Buy
feed.summary: This article identifies the 10 best tech stocks to buy now, according to Morningstar analysts, highlighting companies that are undervalued in a largely overvalued sector. These firms, including Sensata Technologies, STMicroelectronics, and Adobe, possess narrow or wide economic moats and are trading significantly below their fair value estimates. The analysis provides in-depth commentary on each company's competitive advantages, growth drivers, and market position.

feed.title: Baya Systems raises $36M to transform AI chiplet design
feed.summary: Baya Systems, a system-on-chip design startup, has secured $36 million in Series B funding to advance its software for the emerging "chiplet" economy. The company aims to address challenges in AI chiplet design by developing a "revolutionary" chiplet-optimized network-on-chip (NoC) interconnect solution. This technology creates a unified fabric for NoC-to-NoC communications, improving efficiency and flexibility for complex multi-die designs required by AI.

feed.title: AMDG Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:AMDG
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Leverage Shares 2X Long AMD Daily ETF (AMDG), including its current price, performance metrics, key statistics, and investment strategy. It highlights that AMDG is a leveraged ETF designed for short-term bullish bets on AMD, aiming for 200% daily exposure, and emphasizes its high-risk nature and potential for significant deviation due to compounding if held long-term. The article also includes analysis of its holdings, technical indicators suggesting a strong sell, and answers frequently asked questions about ETFs and AMDG's specifics.

feed.title: NYSEARCA:NVDY: High-Yield Income ETF or Missed NVIDIA Gains?
feed.summary: The YieldMax NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (NYSEARCA:NVDY) offers an attractive 84% dividend yield through a covered call strategy on NVIDIA stock. While it provides high monthly income, this approach caps upside potential, leading to significant underperformance compared to NVIDIA's direct stock appreciation. Investors must weigh NVDY's income generation against its limited total return, dividend volatility, and specific risks like single-stock dependence and tax inefficiency.

feed.title: AI stock rout: The US selloff in 4 charts
feed.summary: US stocks experienced a selloff, particularly in large-cap growth, following news that Chinese AI startup DeepSeek developed an advanced AI model that requires less sophisticated semiconductor chips. This development triggered concerns about the necessity of significant capital expenditures by large US tech companies in generative AI, leading to a 17% plunge in Nvidia stock and declines in other chipmakers and utility stocks. However, some tech giants like Apple and Meta Platforms saw gains, and Morningstar analysts maintain their fair value estimates for Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet, expecting long-term benefits from reduced AI model pricing.

feed.title: Amplify Revamps Video Game Tech ETF With New Index
feed.summary: Amplify has updated its Video Game Tech ETF (GAMR) to track the VettaFi Video Game Leaders Index (VGAME) as of January 28, 2025. The ETF, with $40 million in assets, provides exposure to global video gaming companies, including hardware, software, and development platforms. The new index is concentrated, comprising 20 companies with a significant portion based in the U.S., Japan, and China, and features major players like NVIDIA, Meta, and Microsoft.

feed.title: Mawson Infrastructure Group signs 20MW lease with AI customer
feed.summary: Mawson Infrastructure Group has secured a 20MW lease with an undisclosed AI customer for deploying Nvidia GPUs, with a Letter of Intent for a potential expansion to 144MW. The company is also planning to increase its total capacity to 153MW through an expansion in Ohio. This move signifies Mawson's strategic shift towards AI and high-performance computing markets, building on its growing digital colocation business.

feed.title: Chipotle (CMG) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
feed.summary: Chipotle (CMG) is expected to report its earnings tomorrow, with analysts projecting a 13.3% year-on-year revenue growth to $2.85 billion and adjusted earnings of $0.25 per share. The company previously missed revenue expectations by 0.8% last quarter, despite a narrow beat on EBITDA. Sentiment among restaurant sector investors is positive, though Chipotle's stock has seen a slight decline recently.

feed.title: Intel Announces Another 58 Layoffs For Folsom Campus
feed.summary: Intel has announced an additional 58 layoffs at its Folsom campus, continuing its cost-cutting measures due to stagnant sales and increased competition. This new round of job cuts, expected by March 31, follows previous significant reductions, with the Folsom workforce decreasing from over 6,000 in 2018 to under 4,000. The company is also planning to sell its 150-acre Folsom campus in a sale-leaseback deal as part of its broader restructuring efforts and aims to cut approximately 15,000 jobs globally.

feed.title: AMD shares drop more than 6%, close at lowest since 2023 on disappointing data center revenue
feed.summary: AMD's shares dropped over 6% after the company's data center business underperformed Wall Street's revenue estimates, despite overall better-than-expected top and bottom-line results. The chipmaker reported data center sales of $3.86 billion, falling short of the $4.14 billion analysts anticipated. Following this, several Wall Street firms, including Citi and JPMorgan, trimmed their price targets on AMD, citing concerns about competition from Nvidia and potential first-half weakness.

feed.title: VEA vs VEU: Which International Vanguard ETF Is the Better Buy Today?
feed.summary: This article compares two international Vanguard ETFs, VEA (Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets ETF) and VEU (Vanguard FTSE All-World ex-US ETF), to help investors decide which is a better buy. It details their holdings, sector weightings, and financial metrics, highlighting key differences such as VEU's larger allocation to emerging markets and significant positions in Taiwan Semiconductor and Tencent Holdings, which could offer a growth advantage over VEA.

feed.title: Building tiny chips that can handle enormous data
feed.summary: This article explores the advancements in computing, focusing on quantum computing and nanotechnology, to handle the vast data demands of AI. It highlights IBM's research into developing powerful yet energy-efficient chips that move beyond traditional binary systems, aiming for significantly increased processing power and efficiency in the near future. The piece also corrects some initial misinterpretations of IBM's complex technology, clarifying the distinct but potentially integrated roles of quantum computing and AI.

feed.title: Amkor Technology Reports Financial Results for the Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024
feed.summary: Amkor Technology, Inc. announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. For the fourth quarter, net sales were $1.63 billion and net income was $106 million, while full-year net sales reached $6.32 billion with a net income of $354 million. The company highlighted record revenue in its computing end market despite weakness in automotive, industrial, and communications sectors, and provided guidance for the first quarter of 2025.

feed.title: Digihost to Develop High-Performance Computing and AI-Tier
feed.summary: Digihost Technology Inc. announced the formation of US Data Centers, Inc., a new subsidiary dedicated to developing high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence (AI)-focused data centers. Their first project involves transforming an existing site in Columbiana, Alabama, into a 55 MW Tier 3 data center with an estimated total capital expenditure of $440 million, planned for completion by Q1 2027. This strategic investment aims to meet the escalating demand for AI-driven computing power and cloud services.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries Reports Fourth Quarter 2024 and Fiscal Year 2024 Financial Results
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) announced its preliminary financial results for Q4 2024 and the full fiscal year 2024, exceeding guidance for the quarter despite industry challenges. The company reported over $1 billion in Non-IFRS adjusted free cash flow for 2024 and highlighted strong design win momentum for 2025. GF also detailed a significant impairment charge related to legacy assets in Malta, New York, and shared recent business milestones including new partnerships and advanced packaging developments.

feed.title: Is Intel now a Trump stock? Its shares surge after upbeat comments by J.D. Vance.
feed.summary: Shares of Intel Corp. surged by 6% after Vice President J.D. Vance made bullish comments about semiconductor manufacturing in the U.S. at an AI summit in Paris. This positive outlook on U.S. chip making also benefited GlobalFoundries Inc., whose shares also jumped over 6%. The article suggests a potential political alignment for Intel's stock given Vance's comments.

feed.title: SMCY Is A High-Risk, High-Reward Bet On AI (NYSEARCA:SMCY)
feed.summary: The article discusses SMCY as a high-risk, high-reward investment in AI, benefiting from Nvidia's growth and Super Micro Computer's potential despite management issues. It highlights SMCI's undervaluation and expected earnings growth, and the high-yield income potential of the YieldMax SMCI Option Income Strategy ETF (SMCY) through covered calls. The author holds a 3% portfolio position in SMCY, anticipating potential outperformance if SMCI's earnings are positive.

feed.title: New ETFs From Direxion Focus on Daily AMD Results
feed.summary: Direxion has launched two new single-stock ETFs focusing on Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), offering distinct leveraged and inverse strategies. These actively managed funds, with an expense ratio of 97 basis points, aim to capture investor interest in AMD's daily performance, especially given the company's role in the AI race. They are designed for traders and investors who understand the high-risk, high-reward nature of daily leveraged and inverse products.

feed.title: Schwab Expands 24-Hour Trading to All Retail Clients
feed.summary: Charles Schwab is expanding its overnight trading services to all retail clients, allowing them to trade a wider range of securities 24 hours a day, five days a week. This move follows a successful pilot program and aims to meet the demand from investors keen to react to market-moving events promptly. The expansion includes trading in S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100 companies, as well as hundreds of ETFs.

feed.title: Solid Earnings Reflect Cirrus Logic's (NASDAQ:CRUS) Strength As A Business
feed.summary: Cirrus Logic's recent strong earnings are supported by its impressive free cash flow. The company's accrual ratio of -0.11 indicates that its free cash flow of US$457m significantly exceeded its statutory profit of US$305.1m for the year ending December 2024. Additionally, its earnings per share have grown by 28% annually over the past three years, suggesting strong underlying business performance.

feed.title: Direxion Launches 2 Leveraged AMD ETFs Amid AI Chip Race
feed.summary: Direxion has launched two new leveraged ETFs, Direxion Daily AMD Bull 2X Shares (AMUU) and Direxion Daily AMD Bear 1X Shares (AMDD), offering traders concentrated exposure to Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock. This move comes as AMD expands its presence in the artificial intelligence and data center markets, aiming to challenge Nvidia's dominance. The ETFs are designed for short-term trading due to their high-risk nature.

feed.title: Direxion Daily AMD Bull 2X Shares AMUU ETF Stock Price, Quote, History and News - Webull
feed.summary: This page provides detailed information on the Direxion Daily AMD Bull 2X Shares (AMUU) ETF, including its current stock price, historical data, and recent news. Key financial metrics such as its 52-week high/low, total assets, and year-to-date yield are presented. The page also lists recent weekly performance reports for the AMUU ETF.

feed.title: Report: Arm to build its own chips, and Meta is first in line to buy them
feed.summary: Arm Holdings Plc. reportedly plans to design and build its own server CPUs, with Meta Platforms Inc. lined up as a first customer, marking a significant shift from its traditional licensing model. This move, driven by the lucrative AI chip market, could put Arm in direct competition with its existing licensees, potentially alienating customers but offering substantial new revenue streams. Analysts highlight both the immense opportunity in AI and the risks of disrupting established customer relationships.

feed.title: After Earnings, Is Advanced Micro Devices Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported strong fourth-quarter results with growth in client and data center revenue, though its AI GPU growth forecast was disappointing compared to competitor Nvidia. Morningstar has cut its fair value estimate for AMD to $140 per share from $160 due to less optimism about AI GPU growth, but still considers the stock undervalued. The company holds a narrow economic moat based on intangible assets in chip design and expects significant long-term growth, particularly in the data center segment for AI applications.

feed.title: Direxion Daily AMD Bull 2X Shares Review and Aggregate Rating (AMUU)
feed.summary: MarketBeat has provided an aggregate rating and price target for Direxion Daily AMD Bull 2X Shares (AMUU). The ETF holds a "Moderate Buy" rating with an aggregate price target of $265.08, indicating a potential 6.78% upside. This rating is based on 44 analyst ratings for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), which constitutes 14.5% of the ETF's portfolio.

feed.title: Themes Generative Artificial Intelligence ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (WISE) $WISE
feed.summary: This article provides a review and aggregate rating for the Themes Generative Artificial Intelligence ETF (WISE). MarketBeat calculates the ETF's aggregate rating and price target by considering the weighted analyst ratings and price targets of its underlying holdings. The WISE ETF currently holds an aggregate "Hold" rating with an aggregate price target of $39.62, based on analyst coverage for 75.8% of its portfolio.

feed.title: HIVE Digital Technologies Welcomes Craig Tavares as President and Chief Operating Officer of Buzz HPC to Lead Growth in HPC and GPU Cloud Services
feed.summary: HIVE Digital Technologies has appointed Craig Tavares as President and Chief Operating Officer of Buzz HPC, its GPU Cloud and High-Performance Computing data center business. Tavares brings over 20 years of experience in digital infrastructure and is tasked with scaling Buzz HPC’s business, aiming for $100M in revenue. The company also announced incentive grants of 1,090,000 Restricted Share Units (RSUs) to select employees, including Tavares, to align interests with long-term growth.

feed.title: GTA VI PC Might Launch Early Next Year, According to Corsair
feed.summary: Corsair Gaming CEO Andrew Paul hinted that the PC version of Grand Theft Auto VI (GTA VI) might launch in early 2026, much sooner than PC fans typically expect for Rockstar titles following console releases. While not official confirmation, this suggests a potentially shorter waiting period—around six months—compared to previous Grand Theft Auto games. Console versions of GTA VI are still expected in Fall 2025.

feed.title: Direxion每日一倍放空AMD ETF-AMDD-ETF報酬分析
feed.summary: This article analyzes the performance of the Direxion Daily 1X Short AMD ETF (AMDD), providing detailed return data across various timeframes, including daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly performance. It also lists key metrics such as Alpha, Beta, and R-squared, although these are currently unavailable for this ETF. The data presented aims to help investors assess the past performance and risk characteristics of AMDD.

feed.title: GLOBALFOUNDRIES Inc. (GFS): Among the Semiconductor Stocks Making Impressive Comeback from DeepSeek AI Dip
feed.summary: The article discusses the resurgence of semiconductor stocks, specifically highlighting GLOBALFOUNDRIES Inc. (GFS), after a dip caused by the DeepSeek AI announcement. It frames this comeback within the broader US-China technological competition in AI and semiconductors, noting export restrictions and China's efforts to develop competing technologies. GFS performance has been strong, with an 11.27% gain in the last week, bolstered by an analyst upgrade and optimistic forecasts about market recovery and geopolitical advantages.

feed.title: Micron Redefines Performance for AI PCs, Gamers and Professionals
feed.summary: Micron Technology announced the new Micron 4600 PCIe Gen5 NVMe SSD, designed to significantly boost performance for AI PCs, gamers, creators, and professionals. Leveraging Micron G9 TLC NAND, this SSD delivers sequential read speeds of 14.5 GB/s and write speeds of 12.0 GB/s, effectively halving AI model loading times compared to Gen4 SSDs and offering improved energy efficiency. The 4600 SSD is compatible with leading processors from AMD and Intel and integrates advanced security features.

feed.title: Microsoft’s Majorana 1 chip carves new path for quantum computing - Source
feed.summary: Microsoft unveiled Majorana 1, the world's first quantum chip utilizing a new Topological Core architecture, aiming to achieve industrial-scale quantum computing within years. This breakthrough leverages topoconductors and topological qubits, offering a path to scalable quantum systems with millions of qubits to solve complex global problems. The technology addresses previous challenges in qubit stability and control, positioning Microsoft as a leader in the race for practical quantum computing.

feed.title: Micron Stock Soars 26% YTD: Should You Buy More or Book Profits?
feed.summary: Micron Technology's stock has surged 26.5% year-to-date in 2025, driven by strong AI demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and strategic partnerships, including with NVIDIA. However, the article suggests short-term risks such as margin contraction, weak consumer demand in PC and smartphone markets, and geopolitical tensions with China, making it potentially prudent to book profits. Despite long-term AI growth potential, near-term challenges and profitability concerns are highlighted.

feed.title: Going Into Earnings, Is Nvidia Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
feed.summary: Morningstar assesses Nvidia (NVDA) stock ahead of its Q4 earnings report, maintaining a "fairly valued" rating with a long-term fair value estimate of $130 per share. The analysis highlights the critical role of Nvidia's data center business, its wide economic moat due to GPU technology and CUDA software, and the "Very High" uncertainty rating given competitive pressures and market dynamics. Despite potential slowdown concerns and competition, Morningstar projects robust growth for Nvidia's data center revenue in the near-to-medium term.

feed.title: Arteris Revolutionizes Semiconductor Design with FlexGen – Smart Network-on-Chip IP Delivering Unprecedented Productivity Improvements and Quality of Results
feed.summary: Arteris has introduced FlexGen, a smart network-on-chip (NoC) interconnect IP designed to significantly accelerate chip development and optimize performance efficiency. This technology offers up to a 10x productivity boost, a 30% reduction in wire length, and improved latency, addressing the growing demand for faster, more sustainable innovation across various industries. FlexGen's AI-driven automation reduces manual adjustments by over 90%, enabling rapid generation of optimized NoC topologies and maintaining high quality for complex semiconductor designs.

feed.title: Complex AI chips will drive demand for Lam Research chipmaking tools, CEO says
feed.summary: Lam Research CEO Tim Archer predicts that growing demand for complex AI silicon will significantly increase orders from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) for their chipmaking tools over the next three years. The company projects revenue between US$25 billion and US$28 billion in 2028, up from US$16.2 billion in 2024, and announced two new groundbreaking tools: one that uses molybdenum instead of tungsten for material deposition and another for more precise etching. These developments underscore the semiconductor industry's reliance on companies like Lam Research for advanced manufacturing capabilities.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD): Among the Best Long-Term Stocks to Buy According to Ken Fisher
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) is listed among Ken Fisher's best long-term stocks to buy, as identified from Fisher Asset Management's 13F portfolio. Fisher, a renowned investment analyst and founder of Fisher Investments, maintains a significant stake in AMD. Despite AMD's promising outlook and strong 2024 financial performance, the article suggests other stocks might offer higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

feed.title: Chip design software maker Cadence revenue skyrockets with AI demand
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems reported strong Q4 2024 revenues, up 27% to $1.36 billion, driven by surging demand for AI-driven chip design software and expanded partnerships with major chipmakers like Nvidia and Intel. Despite robust growth and a 2024 fiscal year revenue of $4.6 billion, the company's 2025 financial outlook fell below Wall Street expectations due to uncertainties in its China business amid evolving trade restrictions. Cadence expects flat growth in China for 2025 and plans to be prudent with its guidance given the market's unpredictability.

feed.title: Should You Pick Unity Stock At $28?
feed.summary: Unity Software (NYSE: U) released Q4 results showing a loss of $0.30 per share on $457 million in sales, beating analyst estimates. Despite a 25% year-over-year revenue decline, driven by drops in its Create Solutions and Grow Solutions segments, Unity stock jumped 30% to $28 after the company announced its new AI-powered advertising network, Vector. The article suggests that while the stock appears fully priced at 6x price-to-sales and faces near-term headwinds, the new AI platform offers future growth potential.

feed.title: Apple’s $500B US expansion includes chips from TSMC Phoenix
feed.summary: Apple pledged $500 billion for US expansion, including sourcing chips from TSMC's Phoenix factory, where mass production began recently. This investment is set to create 20,000 new US jobs and expand manufacturing and data centers across several states. Former President Trump and the Biden administration both claim credit for stimulating domestic chip production, with Trump citing tariffs and Biden highlighting the CHIPS and Science Act.

feed.title: Micron starts shipping 1γ DRAMs ...
feed.summary: Micron Technology has announced it has begun shipping 1γ (1-gamma) 16Gbit DDR5 DRAMs to partners and customers, claiming to be the first to reach this sixth generation of DRAM process technology. This new memory offers up to a 15 percent speed increase and more than 20 percent power reduction compared to its predecessor, with a 30 percent increase in bits-per-wafer, though it requires EUV lithography. Micron plans to integrate 1γ across its DRAM range for various applications, including data centers, AI-PCs, mobile products, and automotive.

feed.title: Nvidia to report earnings amid infrastructure spending, DeepSeek concerns
feed.summary: Nvidia is set to release its fourth-quarter earnings, capping off a remarkable year of growth driven by demand for its data center GPUs essential for AI. Despite its success, investors are cautious due to concerns about major customers potentially tightening capital expenditure and the emergence of more efficient AI models from startups like DeepSeek, which could reduce the need for extensive GPU infrastructure. CEO Jensen Huang is expected to address these concerns, emphasizing the continued need for GPUs in AI development and deployment, particularly for the inference phase.

feed.title: Navitas Semiconductor Full Year 2024 Earnings: Misses Expectations
feed.summary: Navitas Semiconductor (NASDAQ:NVTS) reported its Full Year 2024 earnings, showing a revenue of US$83.3m, an increase of 4.8% from FY 2023, and a net loss of US$84.6m, a 42% reduction from the previous year. Both revenue and earnings per share missed analyst estimates by 1.3% and 17% respectively. The company's shares dropped 28% in the past week, and analysts forecast a 21% annual revenue growth for the next three years.

feed.title: Top Distribution Execs Are Grappling With Financial Impact Of Tariffs
feed.summary: Top executives from leading technology distributors like Ingram Micro, Arrow Electronics, TD Synnex, and D&H Distributing are preparing for the financial impact of new tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump. While they anticipate increased costs will be passed down to end-users, questions remain about price elasticity and potential delays in purchases. Distributors are leveraging past experiences, adjusting supply chains, and focusing on transparent communication with partners to navigate the evolving situation.

feed.title: Focus on Execution as Synopsys Reports Solid 1Q FY 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: Synopsys reported solid 1Q FY 2025 financial results, with revenue of $1.455 billion andGAAP EPS of $1.89, demonstrating strong performance driven by design activity and its قیادت in various technology sectors. The company's focus on hardware-assisted verification, EDA software, and AI innovations, particularly for 2nm projects, positions it for continued growth. Synopsys expects 2Q FY 2025 revenue between $1.585 billion and $1.615 billion, anticipating a 10.0% year-over-year growth.

feed.title: Sanan-STMicro JV Finishes Building USD3 Billion SiC Wafer Factory in Chongqing
feed.summary: A joint venture between Chinese semiconductor firm Sanan Optoelectronics and Swiss company STMicroelectronics has completed construction of a CNY23 billion (USD3.2 billion) silicon carbide (SiC) wafer factory in Chongqing. The factory, China's first of its kind, is set to begin mass production of 8-inch automotive-grade SiC electronic control chips in Q4, with an expected annual output of 480,000 units. This initiative aims to localize SiC chip production for new energy vehicles in China, anticipating a significant reduction in costs and market growth to USD6.1 billion by 2030.

feed.title: Is Advanced Micro Devices Stock Underperforming the Nasdaq?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock has significantly underperformed the Nasdaq Composite, falling 26.7% over the past three months and 43.4% over 52 weeks. This underperformance is attributed to competition, particularly from Chinese AI models and NVIDIA, and concerns about its AI market share. Despite beating Q4 revenue and EPS estimates, analysts are moderately bullish with a "Moderate Buy" rating, projecting a 47.8% potential upside.

feed.title: BioXcel Therapeutics Soars: Analyzing Sustained Gains
feed.summary: BioXcel Therapeutics saw its stock soar by over 108% following its regaining compliance with Nasdaq's minimum closing bid price rule. This compliance has significantly boosted investor confidence, despite the company's financial results showing substantial operating losses and negative equity. While the market response is optimistic, the article highlights the importance of strategic improvements and operational efficiency to translate this momentum into sustainable financial viability.

feed.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC announces new $100B investment in US
feed.summary: Taiwanese chip giant TSMC announced a new $100 billion investment in the U.S. for three additional advanced semiconductor manufacturing plants, bringing their total commitment to $165 billion. CEO C.C. Wei credited President Donald Trump's vision for these investments, which will include an R&D center in Arizona. This expansion follows TSMC's existing $65 billion commitment under the CHIPS and Science Act and comes amidst discussions about avoiding potential U.S. tariffs on foreign-made chips.

feed.title: Microchip Technology to cut 2,000 chip jobs, will close Arizona fab earlier than planned
feed.summary: Microchip Technology is laying off around 2,000 employees and closing its Arizona chip fab early as part of a cost-cutting and restructuring effort. The company expects to incur $30 million to $40 million in restructuring costs but anticipates annual savings of $90 million to $100 million. This move follows five successive quarters of revenue decline and a pause in its CHIPS Act funding application.

feed.title: Malaysia-Arm Deal To Drive Semiconductor Growth
feed.summary: Malaysia's partnership with United Kingdom-based Arm Holdings Plc (ARM) involves a US$250 million investment to develop proprietary intellectual property (IP) for integrated circuit (IC) design, aiming to elevate Malaysia into a high-value economy and foster local semiconductor firm growth. This collaboration, believed to be the world's first between a nation and a technology giant for IP development, is expected to accelerate the formation of front-end semiconductor firms and help achieve a semiconductor export target of RM1.2 trillion by 2030. Industry analysts have offered mixed outlooks, with some maintaining 'neutral' stances due to current market conditions but acknowledging long-term positive spillover effects for the technology sector.

feed.title: Chipmakers are finding that good earnings aren't enough to please Wall Street
feed.summary: Despite the ongoing AI boom, chipmakers like Marvell Technology, Nvidia, and Advanced Micro Devices are struggling to impress Wall Street, as investor expectations have reached historically high levels. Even with strong earnings and revenue, many stocks have plunged because guidance or performance fell short of optimistic projections. This trend highlights the immense pressure on semiconductor companies amidst the AI buildout and increasing scrutiny from investors.

feed.title: Synopsys Introduces Virtualizer Native Execution on Arm Hardware to Accelerate Software-defined Product Development
feed.summary: Synopsys has launched Virtualizer Native Execution on Arm-based hardware to speed up software development for edge devices across automotive, high-performance computing, and IoT sectors. This innovation enables accelerated virtual prototype execution by leveraging common instruction set architectures between Arm servers and edge devices, integrating into CI/CD pipelines, and supporting hybrid prototyping for comprehensive system-level validation. The solution promises faster validation, earlier software development, and improved performance analysis for software-defined products.
